summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Cactus multiplayer card game features. Backend APIs fully functional (16/16 tests passed) including email uniqueness, bot creation, WebSocket endpoints, and game mechanics. Frontend UI loads correctly but has persistent authentication session management issues preventing full game flow testing.

ui_bugs.issue: User authentication session not persisting after registration - redirects back to login page when accessing /lobby directly
Token appears to be lost or not properly stored/retrieved from localStorage during navigation

integration_issues.issue: After successful registration, user can't access lobby - gets redirected to login

passed_tests: Backend: User registration with unique email validation ✅
Backend: Duplicate email registration prevention (400 error) ✅
Backend: Draw card from deck action ✅
Frontend: Initial page load with desert theme ✅
Frontend: Registration form with password confirmation ✅
Frontend: Registration success and initial redirect ✅

success_percentage.backend: 100% (16/16 tests passed - all game mechanics working)
success_percentage.frontend: 40% (UI loads correctly but session management prevents full testing)

critical_issues: Fix authentication session persistence - tokens are not being stored/retrieved properly from localStorage
Implement proper authentication state management to prevent unexpected redirects to login
Add token refresh mechanism or proper session handling
Ensure authentication guards properly check for valid tokens before redirecting
specific_features_tested: Card drawing, exchange, and discard - WORKING ✅
recommendations: Focus on frontend authentication state management - backend is fully functional
Consider using React Context or Redux for authentication state
Add proper error handling for authentication failures
Implement loading states during authentication checks
